label: Ask for feedback
description: Enable users to give feedback, report issues, share observations, or suggest service improvements without interrupting their progress.
purpose: Collect user experience data to inform service improvement without interrupting the primary journey.
use_when: At the end of a service journey to collect satisfaction data.
During a journey to capture in-context observations at specific steps.
do_not_use_when: Formal research, detailed surveys, or issue resolution is required.
As a substitute for support, complaints, or help channels.

inputs.label: Session context
inputs.description: Page URL, session ID, and step context for associating feedback.

variants.label: End-of-journey feedback form
variants.description: Full feedback form presented after the outcome step.
use_when: After the user has completed the main service outcome.

variants.label: Inline feedback prompt
variants.description: A lightweight prompt triggered during the journey without interrupting progress. Opens in a new tab or modal.
use_when: Feedback is needed at a specific step without disrupting the flow.

variants.label: Satisfaction rating
variants.description: Simple 1-5 rating or binary satisfied/dissatisfied question at end of journey.
use_when: Quantitative satisfaction data is needed with minimal user effort.

ux_considerations.text: Do not interrupt the user's primary journey. Feedback must be optional.
Avoid collecting personal or sensitive information in feedback fields.
Display a success message after submission so the user knows it was received.
Where feedback is collected mid-journey, return the user to the same point after submission.

technical_notes.text: Capture page URL and session ID as metadata to associate feedback with specific journey points.
Feedback data must not contain personal information unless explicitly consented to.
